zone_6b_notes: Plant bare-root crowns in early spring (late March to April) in Farmington. June-bearing varieties produce one large crop over 2â€“3 weeks in late May to June â€” the most flavorful type, ideal for fresh eating, jam, and freezing. Matted row system: plant 18 inches apart, allow runners to fill in to 12-inch-wide rows. Remove flower buds the first year to allow plants to establish. Mulch heavily with straw in winter (3â€“4 inches) after hard freeze. Renovate beds after harvest each year: mow to 4 inches, thin to 4â€“5 plants per foot, fertilize. Earliglow, Allstar, and Jewel are standard reliable varieties for Zone 6b Missouri. Ideal for home gardeners in Missouri, requiring minimal investment and producing good crops within 13 months with proper cultivar selection.
